screens.name: Songs workspace
screens.purpose: Help a solo user scan copied songs and inspect one digitized score without losing list context.
screens.primary_action: Select a song from the library

ux_rules: Keep the workspace in a two-column list-detail layout on large screens and stack the list above the detail pane on smaller screens.
Treat song selection as the dominant action in the list region and avoid introducing competing primary actions in the same plane.
Preserve a visible empty state in the detail pane before any song is selected.
Make the empty detail pane feel intentional and informative rather than like an unfinished blank canvas.
Use warm muted surfaces and restrained accents so hierarchy comes from grouping and contrast rather than saturated highlights.
Keep radius values restrained and avoid friendly SaaS-style pill treatments unless they carry real state meaning.
Keep metadata compact and scannable at both the list item and selected detail header levels.
Prefer divider-led grouping over stacking too many inset cards inside already bounded surfaces.
Render score sections as readable blocks with stable rhythm for chord lines and lyric lines.

screen_contexts.layout_intent: Use the list-detail workspace shell so the library remains scannable while one score expands in the adjacent pane.
interaction_risks: Selected item may become visually ambiguous if the list styling is too muted.
The page can drift into marketing-shell spacing if the header grows larger than the working content.
Score blocks can lose readability if metadata and notation surfaces use too many competing treatments.
Too many nested bordered rectangles can make the page feel like a card stack instead of a calm workspace.
An oversized empty detail pane can read as accidental whitespace instead of a deliberate waiting state.
notes: Loading and error states are deferred because the MVP uses synchronous local song data.
The detail-screen template still applies for grouping, contextual clarity, and supporting metadata.

constraints: Keep selection state in the workspace surface that owns the list.
Use one dominant selected treatment in the list and do not add extra primary CTAs to the list panel.
Favor warm neutral fills, understated borders, restrained radius, and low-noise material treatment across page, list, and detail regions.
Avoid rounded-full badges and oversized corner radii that make the workspace feel softer than the selected aesthetic profile.
Use separators and typography hierarchy before introducing another contained sub-card.
Keep the page header compact enough that the working panes remain the dominant landmarks on first view.
Maintain explicit empty states for both the list region and the detail region where relevant.
open_decisions: Whether to introduce a dedicated token layer beyond current Tailwind classes remains undecided.
Search and filtering controls are intentionally deferred until the library size requires them.

critical_judgments: The list-detail layout should preserve workflow throughput over ornamental whitespace.
The selected song must remain obvious without relying on bright accents.
Score section rhythm should feel calm and readable while keeping practical density.
must_not_regress: Explicit empty state before initial selection
Persistent visibility of the song list while the detail pane is active
Quiet warm palette and restrained surface treatment
